settings.label: Bundle

settings.desc: Rebuild what stress, pollution, and harsh products have stripped. A complete protocol for long-term barrier health.

settings.title: Identify your primary concern
settings.body: Start with one concern — hydration, brightness, texture, or sensitivity. Layering too many active formulas at once reduces effectiveness and can irritate skin.

settings.title: Check for active conflicts
settings.body: Some actives don't mix well. AHAs and retinol should be used on separate evenings. Vitamin C pairs well with SPF in the morning but avoid layering it with niacinamide at high concentrations.

settings.title: Start slow, build gradually
settings.body: Introduce new actives every 2 weeks. Begin with every-other-night use and build up as your skin adjusts. Patch test all new products before full application.

settings.title: Layer in the right order
settings.body: Apply thinnest to thickest — water-based serums first, then oil-based, then moisturiser. Allow 60 seconds between layers for full absorption.

settings.quote: I've tried dozens of serums and nothing has come close to the Hydra-Boost. My skin looks visibly plumper and I've had three people ask what I'm doing differently. It's simple — it just works.
settings.author: Margot Ellison

settings.quote: The Barrier Repair Serum genuinely saved my skin after a bad reaction to a prescription retinoid. Redness gone in four days. I don't leave home without it now.
settings.author: Théa Beaumont

settings.quote: The Calm Routine bundle is hands-down the best skincare purchase I've made. My rosacea is under control for the first time in years. Worth every penny.
settings.author: Aoife Kennelly

settings.question: Can I use more than one serum at the same time?
settings.answer: Yes — most Velour Skin serums are designed to layer. Apply in order of lightest to heaviest texture, and allow 60 seconds between each for absorption. Avoid layering AHAs with retinol on the same evening.

settings.question: How do I know which serum is right for my skin?
settings.answer: Start with your primary concern — whether that's hydration, texture, sensitivity, or glow. Use our filter above to narrow down by concern, or take our Skin Quiz for a personalised recommendation.

settings.question: Are these serums suitable for sensitive skin?
settings.answer: All Velour Skin formulas are fragrance-free and developed with sensitive skin in mind. For highly reactive skin, we recommend the Barrier Repair or Calm Serums as your first step. Always patch test before full facial use.

settings.question: Do I need to use SPF with these serums?
settings.answer: Yes. Any formula containing Vitamin C, AHAs, or retinol increases photosensitivity. We recommend a broad-spectrum SPF 30+ every morning, regardless of which serum you use.

settings.question: How long will results take?
settings.answer: Hydration and soothing benefits are often noticeable within 24–48 hours. Brighter, more even skin from Vitamin C or AHAs typically takes 3–4 weeks with consistent use. Retinol and peptide results are best assessed at 8–12 weeks.

settings.question: Are your products vegan and cruelty-free?
settings.answer: Yes. All Velour Skin products are 100% vegan and certified cruelty-free. We do not test on animals at any stage of production, and we do not sell in markets that require animal testing.
